name: Yankee Lamb Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Pat the lamb dry and sprinkle with salt and pepper as desired.
3. Heat oil in a large covered casserole or Dutch oven over high heat on top of the stove.
4. Add lamb, without crowding, in batches if necessary, and brown well on all sides. Remove pieces to a plate as they are done and reserve. Repeat until all lamb is browned.
5. Pour off any remaining fat and replace casserole over medium heat.
6. Add onion, half the carrots, celery, garlic, and tomato paste. Cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Use your spoon to loosen and dissolve the brown bits stuck to the bottom of the casserole.
8. Add flour and cook an additional minute, stirring.
9. Add wine and cook for about a minute to burn off the alcohol.
10. Replace lamb (and any juices on the plate) in the casserole and add stock, peppercorns, and thyme. Cover, bring to a boil, and place in oven for 1 ¼-to-1 ½ hours, or until meat is tender.
11. Meanwhile, combine potatoes, remaining carrots, butter with 1/2 cup water in a medium saucepan over medium heat and cook for 5 minutes.
12. Add turnips and cook, stirring occasionally, until all water is evaporated, leaving vegetables bathed in butter, about another 5 minutes.
13. Add peas, remove from the heat, and set aside.
14. Remove casserole from oven. Using a slotted spoon, remove meat to a bowl.
15. Pour sauce through a fine strainer into another bowl and discard solids remaining in strainer.
16. Replace meat in the pot, add cooked vegetables and sauce. Cover and place over medium heat for 5 minutes.
17. Serve hot.
